2604.12223.title: LLM-Guided Semantic Bootstrapping for Interpretable Text Classification with Tsetlin Machines
2604.12223.paper_summary: Pretrained language models (PLMs) like BERT provide strong semantic representations but are costly and opaque, while symbolic models such as the Tsetlin Machine (TM) offer transparency but lack semantic generalization. We propose a semantic bootstrapping framework that transfers LLM knowledge into symbolic form, combining interpretability with semantic capacity. Given a class label, an LLM generates sub-intents that guide synthetic data creation through a three-stage curriculum (seed, core, enriched), expanding semantic diversity. A Non-Negated TM (NTM) learns from these examples to extract high-confidence literals as interpretable semantic cues. Injecting these cues into real data enables a TM to align clause logic with LLM-inferred semantics. Our method requires no embeddings or runtime LLM calls, yet equips symbolic models with pretrained semantic priors. Across multiple text classification tasks, it improves interpretability and accuracy over vanilla TM, achieving performance comparable to BERT while remaining fully symbolic and efficient.
2604.12223.paper_summary_zh: 預訓練語言模型（PLMs）如 BERT 提供強大的語義表示，但成本高且不透明，而符號模型如 Tsetlin 機器（TM）則提供透明性但缺乏語義泛化。我們提出了一個語義自舉框架，將 LLM 知識轉換為符號形式，結合可解釋性和語義能力。給定一個類別標籤，LLM 生成子意圖，通過三階段課程（種子、核心、增強）指導合成數據的創建，擴大語義多樣性。非否定 TM（NTM）從這些例子中學習，以提取高置信度的字面量作為可解釋的語義線索。將這些線索注入真實數據使 TM 能夠將子句邏輯與 LLM 推斷的語義對齊。我們的方法不需要嵌入或運行時 LLM 調用，但為符號模型提供了預訓練的語義先驗。在多個文本分類任務中，它提高了可解釋性和準確性，超過了普通 TM，實現了與 BERT 相當的性能，同時保持完全符號化和高效。
2604.12223.author: Jiechao Gao et.al.
2604.12223.authors: Jiechao Gao, Rohan Kumar Yadav, Yuangang Li, Yuandong Pan, Jie Wang, Ying Liu, Michael Lepech
